title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Conflict Triage
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded Git automation and trust policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Safe Directory Ownership evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Safe Directory Ownership assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. Competency probe: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Safe Directory Ownership has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Safe Directory Ownership precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. Competency probe: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Adding wildcard safe.directory is the correct fix for every ownership warning.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Safe Directory Ownership case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Safe Directory Ownership case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Safe Directory Ownership case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Safe Directory Ownership case

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. Competency probe: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Cloning a repository installs and runs all versioned hooks automatically.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects. Artifact under review: a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Hook Lifecycle, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Hook Lifecycle record says a bounded Git automation and trust policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Hook Lifecycle without its stated preconditions, omit the a bounded Git automation and trust policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Hook Lifecycle for a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-safe-directory-ownership-1yta6j" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Directory Ownership into observable performance evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.
